heading: Definitions
label: 2467 Definitions

amendments: Amendments2002—Par. (4)(D). amended subpar. (D) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (D) read as follows: “a minimum age for the employment of children; and”.Pub. L. 107–2102000—Par. (6). added par. (6).Pub. L. 106–200

children.heading: Beneficiary developing country
children.label: 1 Beneficiary developing country
children.text: The term “beneficiary developing country” means any country with respect to which there is in effect an Executive order or Presidential proclamation by the President designating such country as a beneficiary developing country for purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Country
children.label: 2 Country
children.text: The term “country” means any foreign country or territory, including any overseas dependent territory or possession of a foreign country, or the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands. In the case of an association of countries which is a free trade area or customs union, or which is contributing to comprehensive regional economic integration among its members through appropriate means, including, but not limited to, the reduction of duties, the President may by Executive order or Presidential proclamation provide that all members of such association other than members which are barred from designation under shall be treated as one country for purposes of this subchapter.section 2462(b) of this title

children.heading: Entered
children.label: 3 Entered
children.text: The term “entered” means entered, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, in the customs territory of the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: the right to organize and bargain collectively;

children.label: C
children.text: a prohibition on the use of any form of forced or compulsory labor;

children.label: D
children.text: a minimum age for the employment of children, and a prohibition on the worst forms of child labor, as defined in paragraph (6); and

children.label: E
children.text: acceptable conditions of work with respect to minimum wages, hours of work, and occupational safety and health.

children.heading: Least-developed beneficiary developing country
children.label: 5 Least-developed beneficiary developing country
children.text: The term “least-developed beneficiary developing country” means a beneficiary developing country that is designated as a least-developed beneficiary developing country under .section 2462(a)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: all forms of slavery or practices similar to slavery, such as the sale or trafficking of children, debt bondage and serfdom, or forced or compulsory labor, including forced or compulsory recruitment of children for use in armed conflict;

children.label: B
children.text: the use, procuring, or offering of a child for prostitution, for the production of pornography or for pornographic purposes;

children.label: C
children.text: the use, procuring, or offering of a child for illicit activities in particular for the production and trafficking of drugs; and

children.label: D
children.text: work which, by its nature or the circumstances in which it is carried out, is likely to harm the health, safety, or morals of children.
